title: Pipeline Manifest
description: Per-project pipeline configuration for the autopilot pipeline. Lives in <project>/pipeline.yaml. Migrated from CLAUDE.md `pipeline:` block 2026-04-29 — see dotfiles BACKLOG and migration commit for rationale (config belongs in config files, not embedded in markdown prose).

toolchain.description: Tool prerequisites checked at autopilot preflight. Each category is an array of tool names; preflight verifies each is available.

python.description: Python tools (e.g., ruff, mypy, pytest_cov). Verified via `command -v` or `python -c "import <tool>"`.

node.description: Node tools (e.g., eslint, tsc, vitest). Verified via `npx <tool> --version` from frontend directories.

imports.description: Python module imports that must succeed (used by tools beyond what's in `python`). Verified via `python -c "import <module>"`.

infra.description: Infrastructure tools (e.g., bash, jq, sonar-scanner). Verified via `command -v`. Missing tools warn but do not fail preflight.

smoke_test.description: Commands run after merge to main to verify the merge did not break the build. Each command exit-code-checked; non-zero fails the post-merge gate.

integration_test.description: The project's integration *surface*, run UNSANDBOXED by the orchestrator integration gate (real integration gates §4.1). Two forms: (1) legacy flat array of command strings — the detection-half shape; (2) object form declaring commands + trigger globs + services. Each command is timeout-bounded. Gate mode is INTEGRATION_GATE_MODE=warn|deny (default warn).

oneOf.description: Legacy flat form: integration commands only. Equivalent to the object form with no trigger and no services (gate is always eligible, brings up no services).

commands.description: The integration commands the gate runs unsandboxed (e.g. 'bash dashboard/tests/run-all.sh --only-integration').

trigger.description: Path globs (relative to project root) that gate the run (§5). The integration gate fires only if the phase's task diffs touch one of these — the project's declared infra surface. This is the GROUND TRUTH /plan-project reads to author each phase gate's trigger (§4.3); the §7 cross-validation test asserts the authored plan gate matches this by value. Absent ⇒ the gate is always eligible.

oracle_globs.description: Path globs for the integration test/oracle files (and their expected results). During the §4.4 remediation loop the plan-ownership guard WORM-locks these: the lead-developer fixer is structurally DENIED Edit/Write to any matching path (Guard #2, test-immutability). The fixer may add/modify UNIT tests elsewhere (TDD §6b) but never the oracle that judges the gate — a fixer that edits the test produces false green, worse than no gate. Absent ⇒ no WORM lock (the behavioural skill guard still applies).

start_cmd.description: Command that brings the service up inside the ephemeral, isolated, credential-free execution env (§6a Guard #4). Optional.

health_cmd.description: Command that returns 0 once the service is ready (e.g. 'pg_isready -h localhost'). Optional.

services.description: Services the integration suite needs (e.g. a Postgres test DB for OIH). Each is brought up in the ephemeral execution env, not on the host (§6a Guard #4) — this retro-justifies killing the hardcoded `docker compose up db-test` OIH-ism: OIH declares its DB here; dotfiles declares none. Absent ⇒ no services.

contracts.description: Cross-cutting invariants the pipeline enforces (test files that must exist, grep patterns with max-value caps, etc.).

preconditions.description: Declared preconditions checked at autopilot start (warn-level, not blocking). Surfaces tooling gaps upfront so a phase doesn't silently SKIP after 1-2h.

reason.description: Why this precondition matters; printed when check fails.

file.description: Path to package.json or similar (relative to project root).

path.description: Path that must exist (relative to project root).

grinder.description: Per-project configuration for the grinder background quality system. See backlog #2 (zero-tech-debt-pipeline). Optional — projects without grinder coverage skip this block.
